scenario: Consumer 'hidden' adverse event (SHOWSTOPPER) — surface intent looks like an FAQ (brochure/coupon request), but a hidden AE dominates. Must classify as AE, not FAQ.
input_text:
SOURCE CHANNEL: Email Inbound Gateway
PRIMARY DISPATCH LOG: info-reply@cardiomedglobal.com

MESSAGE BODY:
Hi there,

My local pharmacy down on 4th street told me to message your support email directly. I was looking through your public corporate website to try and find the downloadable consumer instructions or a patient voucher coupon for your medication CholoClear-X, but I couldn't find the link anywhere on the main page. Can someone send me the direct website link or an online brochure where I can download that?

Also, while I have you, I wanted to ask if it's normal to get a strange burning sensation in my chest and an irregular, racing heartbeat about an hour after I take the pill? It has happened every morning since I started the new prescription last Thursday, and it makes me feel quite dizzy for a few hours, so I have to sit down at my desk. My doctor is out of town until next week, so I just wanted to read your official patient safety booklet online to see if those are standard issues or if I should stop taking the medication altogether.

Thank you for your help,
Marcus Vance
Home Address: 542 Applewood Lane, Austin, TX
Personal Mobile: 512-555-0144

failed_gates: Gate 3: Conflict margin — top-2 classes within ~0.10 (ambiguous: FAQ surface vs AE)
Gate 4: Adverse-event policy — AE flag set -> PV (never auto-approve)
Gate 6: HCP link — consumer reporter not in HCP registry (expected for a consumer)

labeling_notes: SHOWSTOPPER: The email's surface ask is an FAQ (brochure/coupon link). A naive classifier routes this to Public Health/FAQ Deflection. The hidden adverse event (chest burning, irregular racing heartbeat, dizziness) must dominate -> classify as Adverse Event Report, route to PV. Proves AE detection beats surface classification.

PRIVACY: Reporter is named (Marcus Vance, Consumer). The PATIENT is the same person but must be stored as initials only ('M.V.'), NOT the full name, per HIPAA/GDPR/ICH. contact_email is null (info-reply@cardiomedglobal.com is the COMPANY inbound address, not Marcus's email); only his mobile + home address were given.

SERIOUSNESS — labeled Serious (calibrated decision; originally a coin-flip). The symptoms (chest burning + irregular racing heartbeat + dizziness) read as a possible cardiac arrhythmia. Under ICH E2A this is defensible as life-threatening (criterion b) or an Important Medical Event. We chose Serious over Non-Serious because: (1) the ICH-E2A-grounded prompt lands here STABLY (5/5 runs) once the vague 'medically important' wording was tightened to the standard IME bar; (2) over-calling seriousness errs in the SAFE direction for PV triage, while under-calling a cardiac signal is the dangerous error; (3) the original label was explicitly flagged 'defensible either way'. NOTE: seriousness only affects urgent-vs-standard PV triage — routing to PV is unchanged either way (Gate 4 flags every AE). See PROGRESS calibration story.
model_version / prompt_version are intentionally omitted from ground truth — they are runtime fields the system fills when the agent actually processes the case.
